example_sentences.japanese: 夕飯を食べて、おふろに入って、寝ます。
example_sentences.english: I'll eat dinner, take a bath, and go to bed.

example_sentences.japanese: 一つ、二つ、三つ、四つ、五つ、六つ、七つ、八つ、九つ、十。
example_sentences.english: One, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten.

example_sentences.japanese: 英語は下手でした。
example_sentences.english: I was poor at English.

example_sentences.japanese: 一つ、二つ、三つ、四つ、五つ、六つ、七つ、八つ、九つ、十。
example_sentences.english: One, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten.

example_sentences.japanese: 私はときどき学校を休む。
example_sentences.english: I am absent from school on occasion.

meaning_full: formal writing with little real meaning
part_of_speech: noun or participle which takes the aux. verb suru

example_sentences.japanese: この作文はとてもよく書けてますね。
example_sentences.english: This essay is very well written.

example_sentences.japanese: 借りるのが好きな人は返すのが嫌いだ。
example_sentences.english: Those who like borrowing dislike paying.

example_sentences.japanese: 一つ、二つ、三つ、四つ、五つ、六つ、七つ、八つ、九つ、十。
example_sentences.english: One, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten.

example_sentences.japanese: 一つ、二つ、三つ、四つ、五つ、六つ、七つ、八つ、九つ、十。
example_sentences.english: One, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten.

example_sentences.japanese: 一つ、二つ、三つ、四つ、五つ、六つ、七つ、八つ、九つ、十。
example_sentences.english: One, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten.

example_sentences.japanese: 朝ご飯、食べよっかなぁ。
example_sentences.english: I think I'll have breakfast.

example_sentences.japanese: このペンは太いです。
example_sentences.english: This pen is thick.

part_of_speech: nouns which may take the genitive case particle 'no'

example_sentences.japanese: 左の足が痛いです。
example_sentences.english: My left foot hurts.

example_sentences.japanese: 昨日の晩御飯を食べなかった。
example_sentences.english: I didn't have dinner last night.

example_sentences.japanese: その雑誌は毎週出る。
example_sentences.english: The magazine comes out every week.

example_sentences.japanese: 私は弟が山に登るのが好きではない。
example_sentences.english: I don't like my brother climbing the mountains.

example_sentences.japanese: 夏は、昼が長くて夜が短いです。
example_sentences.english: In the summer, days are long and nights are short.

example_sentences.japanese: 男の人は歯を磨く。
example_sentences.english: The man brushes his teeth.

example_sentences.japanese: 借りるのが好きな人は返すのが嫌いだ。
example_sentences.english: Those who like borrowing dislike paying.

example_sentences.japanese: バスが止まるまで降りるな。
example_sentences.english: Don't get off the bus till it stops.

example_sentences.japanese: あちらが私の待っていた人です。
example_sentences.english: Over there is the person I've been waiting for.

example_sentences.japanese: うーん、いいなあ。そこへ行こう。
example_sentences.english: Hmm, that's a good idea. Let's go there.

example_sentences.japanese: いらねえっつったろ！
example_sentences.english: I told you I don't need it!

example_sentences.japanese: いつ会えんの？
example_sentences.english: When can I meet?

example_sentences.japanese: これはわたしの家のかぎです。
example_sentences.english: This is my house key.

example_sentences.japanese: 「ぼくだいじょうぶ？」といわれました。「だいじょうぶ」とこたえました。
example_sentences.english: I was asked, "You OK, kid?". I replied, "Fine."

meaning_full: range of dishes made with vinegared rice combined with fish, vegetables, egg, etc.

example_sentences.japanese: 私はすしを食べたい。
example_sentences.english: I would like to eat sushi.

example_sentences.japanese: せっけんで手を洗いました。
example_sentences.english: I washed my hands with soap.

example_sentences.japanese: それから先の話を聞きたい。
example_sentences.english: I'd like to know the rest of the story.

part_of_speech: noun or participle which takes the aux. verb suru

example_sentences.japanese: なんちゅう重い机だよ！
example_sentences.english: What a heavy desk this is!

example_sentences.japanese: どちらの道をいっても駅につける。
example_sentences.english: Whichever way you may take, you can get to the station.

example_sentences.japanese: なんでやねん！
example_sentences.english: You must be kidding me!

example_sentences.japanese: 夕飯を食べて、おふろに入って、寝ます。
example_sentences.english: I'll eat dinner, take a bath, and go to bed.

example_sentences.japanese: あの店は安いですが、まずいです。
example_sentences.english: That restaurant is cheap, but the food isn't very good.

example_sentences.japanese: 私の家の近くにりっぱな公園がある。
example_sentences.english: There is a fine park near my house.

meaning_full: angular Japanese syllabary used primarily for loanwords

example_sentences.japanese: ひらがなは書けますが、カタカナはまだ書けません。
example_sentences.english: I can write hiragana, but I can't yet write katakana.

example_sentences.japanese: 私は毎朝三キロ走ります。
example_sentences.english: I run three kilometers every morning.

example_sentences.japanese: ストーブ切った？
example_sentences.english: Did you turn the stove off?

example_sentences.japanese: スプーンとフォークとナイフが要る。どうも。
example_sentences.english: I need a spoon, a fork, and a knife. Thank you.

example_sentences.japanese: スリッパをはいてください。
example_sentences.english: Please put on your slippers.

example_sentences.japanese: 一の前はゼロです。
example_sentences.english: Zero comes before one.

example_sentences.japanese: 私はパンやコーヒーや砂糖などを買った。
example_sentences.english: I bought bread, coffee, sugar and the like.

example_sentences.japanese: これは、私が昨日買ったレコードです。
example_sentences.english: This is the record which I bought yesterday.

meaning_full: to be covered with (dust, snow, etc.)
to bear (e.g. someone's debts, faults, etc.)
to be fogged (due to overexposure, etc.)

example_sentences.japanese: 教室では帽子を被るべきではない。
example_sentences.english: Your hat should not be worn in the classroom.

example_sentences.japanese: カメラを持っていくのを忘れないでください。
example_sentences.english: Don't forget to take a camera with you.

example_sentences.japanese: 今日は、十五夜です。
example_sentences.english: Today is the night of the full moon.
